name: Belvo API
what_it_does: Latin American open finance API for accessing bank account data, transactions, tax information, and employment data across 50+ financial institutions in Mexico, Brazil, and Colombia.
use_cases: Aggregating bank account balances and transactions for Latin American customers
Accessing SAT tax data (Mexico) for income verification and financial analysis
Employment and payroll data retrieval for loan underwriting in LATAM
KYC/KYB verification using official financial institution data
Building personal finance management apps with multi-bank data aggregation
not_for: Non-LATAM financial data (use Plaid for US, TrueLayer for UK/EU)
Payment initiation (Belvo is primarily read-only data access)
Customers without accounts at supported financial institutions
best_when: You're building fintech products for Mexican, Brazilian, or Colombian markets and need programmatic access to bank and tax data.
avoid_when: Your customers are primarily in the US, Europe, or outside Latin America.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth using Secret ID and Secret Password. Credentials tied to your Belvo account. End-user bank credentials are securely proxied through Belvo's widget — not stored by your app.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free sandbox with test accounts; limited live API calls on free plan
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth and Enterprise plans; pricing based on API call volume
pricing.notes: Freemium model. Sandbox access is free. Production access requires a plan. Enterprise pricing negotiated for high volume.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; bank data retrieval is I/O-bound on institution response time

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include code, message, and detail fields. Bank-specific error codes mapped to standard codes. Institution availability issues are surfaced clearly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Link creation and data sync are not idempotent. Agents must track existing links before creating new ones.
known_agent_gotchas: Bank data retrieval is inherently asynchronous and institution-dependent — timeouts are common
Links (user-institution connections) expire and require re-authentication — agents must handle link refresh
Not all data fields are available from all institutions — field availability varies significantly
Sandbox institutions return synthetic data that may not match production behavior
LATAM bank MFA requirements can interrupt automated flows — widget approach needed for user auth
